commands.review_summary:
The PR caps qa-lab Mantis Discord smoke API response body reads with `readResponseWithLimit` and adds an oversized-response fail-closed regression test.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +40. Total +46 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main has an unbounded `response.text()` in the Discord smoke API helper. I did not run a live Discord smoke path in this read-only review.

target.title: improve(qa-lab): bound Discord API response reads in mantis smoke
target.body:
_AI-assisted: implemented with Claude Code. Claude wrote the bounded-read change plus the focused regression test under human direction, and ran the focused vitest suite locally; a human reviewed the diff and the proof before opening._

## What Problem This Solves

The internal mantis Discord smoke check (`qa-lab` extension) talks to `discord.com` and read each response body with an unbounded `await response.text()`. The request goes through `fetchWithSsrFGuard`, but that guard only vets the connection/host — it does not cap the response body. A hostile, compromised, or simply malfunctioning endpoint on the allowlisted host could therefore stream an arbitrarily large payload, and the QA process would buffer the whole thing into memory before `JSON.parse` ever ran. This is defense-in-depth hardening for an operator-facing diagnostic tool: there is no known user-triggered crash today, but the read was the one remaining unbounded path in this file and could OOM the smoke run.

## Why This Change Was Made

Route every Discord response through the shared `readResponseWithLimit` helper (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/response-limit-runtime`, already used by ~20 providers) with a 16 MiB cap — the same constant the qa-lab mock-openai server and several media providers use. The change is deliberately minimal: only the body read is replaced (`const buffer = await readResponseWithLimit(...)` then `buffer.toString("utf8")`); all downstream `JSON.parse`, ok/error handling, and `release()` cleanup are unchanged. The cap is fail-closed: on overflow the helper throws, the existing `runMantisDiscordSmoke` try/catch turns that into `status=fail` + an `error.txt` artifact, so the check degrades gracefully and never posts to Discord. Non-goal: this does not change the SSRF policy, the allowlist, or any legitimate (small JSON) Discord response behavior.

## User Impact

For operators/developers running the mantis Discord smoke check: legitimate Discord JSON responses (well under 16 MiB) behave exactly as before. An abnormally large response now produces a clean `status=fail` with a descriptive `error.txt` ("Discord API <path> response exceeds 16777216 bytes") instead of risking an out-of-memory abort of the smoke run. No public API, CLI flag, or config surface changes.

## Evidence

**Validation Evidence**
- Focused suite: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/qa-lab/src/mantis/discord-smoke.runtime.test.ts` → **7/7 passed** (6 pre-existing + 1 new).
- New test `fails closed when a Discord API response exceeds the byte cap` drives the real `runMantisDiscordSmoke` → `callDiscordApi` with a genuine `new Response` body of `16*1024*1024+1` bytes and asserts: `status==="fail"`, `error.txt` contains `"/users/@me response exceeds"` and `"16777216 bytes"`, zero POST calls (fail-closed before any Discord write), and `release()` still invoked.
- The test exercises the real function, not a mock of it; the only stub is this file's own local `fetchWithSsrFGuard` mock — the shared `provider-http-test-mocks.ts` is intentionally not modified.

**Security & Privacy**
- Removes an unbounded external-body read on the `discord.com` path — a memory-exhaustion / resource-abuse hardening. Fail-closed: overflow aborts the run rather than degrading silently.
- No tokens or target metadata are newly logged; the existing redaction path (`redactMantisDiscordMetadata`) still owns `error.txt` content. The overflow message contains only the request path and the byte limit, no response body.

**Compatibility**
- Backward compatible: identical behavior for all normal-sized Discord responses; `JSON.parse`, status/error handling and connection `release()` are unchanged.
- Reuses the established `readResponseWithLimit` + `onOverflow` pattern (mirrors `extensions/exa/...` and others), so no new dependency or API surface.

Scope is one file (`extensions/qa-lab/src/mantis/discord-smoke.runtime.ts`) plus its test. **Allow edits by maintainers** will be enabled on the PR so maintainers can take it over if needed.
target.author: alix-007
